steps.title: Combine the common denominator
steps.content:
Subtract the second fraction from both sides. Because the two fractions have the same denominator, their numerators combine:
$$\frac{x^2-c^2}{\sqrt{x^2-c^2}}=39$$

steps.title: Solve for the possible values
steps.content:
Square both sides and isolate $x^2$:
$$x^2-c^2=39^2$$
$$x^2=c^2+39^2$$
Thus $x$ can be either square root of $c^2+39^2$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
The matching listed solution is the negative square root:
$$-\sqrt{c^2+39^2}$$
The answer is D.
